claim: Claude Code routines eliminate the need to manage hosting, data persistence, and authentication infrastructure for proactive agents by handling it on managed infrastructure
actionable: Use /schedule command in Claude Code to set up routines instead of building custom cron-based agent infrastructure
source_title: Build a proactive agent workflow with Claude Code

claim: Agent-on-agent review using the generator-critiquer pattern improves documentation quality by having one routine create PRs and another routine reviews them before human review
actionable: Set up two routines where the first generates documentation PRs and the second triggered on PR creation leaves review comments
source_title: Build a proactive agent workflow with Claude Code

claim: Routines support both schedule-based triggers (time-based) and event-based triggers (GitHub events, webhooks) for flexible automation
actionable: Choose schedule-based triggers for regular tasks (e.g., weekly documentation review) and event-based triggers (GitHub issue opens, PR merges) for reactive automation
source_title: Build a proactive agent workflow with Claude Code

claim: Routines sessions are interactive and steerable from web, CLI, and desktop—you can watch, follow up, ask questions, and resume past sessions
actionable: Open a live routine session on claude.ai to steer Claude mid-execution if the agent goes off-track
source_title: Build a proactive agent workflow with Claude Code

claim: When setting up routines, you need to define three core decisions: trigger timing, context/information Claude needs, and how to steer Claude to keep outputs honest
actionable: Before creating a routine, explicitly define when it should run, what repos/tools/docs it needs access to, and what validation hooks you'll use
source_title: Build a proactive agent workflow with Claude Code
